- More trading ⇒ more bonding curve and PolarEX swap fees.
- More POLAR minted ⇒ more conversion gains.

Yield is therefore tied to protocol adoption, not to a counterparty's balance sheet. The mechanism scales with pAsset supply, but realized yield still depends on actual activity, rates, liquidations, contract execution, and market conditions. That is what "self-scaling" means: **activity ⇒ yield**, with no offchain dependency to compress or disappear.

## Owned, not rented

Polaris yield is not subsidized, bridged in, or discretionarily voted into being. It emerges from the protocol's own usage and is captured by the protocol itself — *internalized, not rented*. Because the core is intended to be immutable, no admin key should be able to swap these onchain sources for a T-bill desk under pressure after audited deployment; that architecture is designed to avoid the [degradation pattern](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/why-polaris).

## How yield is distributed

Onchain fees do not flow straight to recipients: conversion proceeds and swap fees go to the **[Fee Router](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship/fee-router)**, which buffers them and distributes pETH yield to troves pro-rata to recorded debt. POLAR Staking rewards and future [vePOLAR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship/vepolar) locker rewards are separate from that pETH stream. The stewarded primary-interest split that funds the Stability Pool, POLAR stakers, and the ecosystem fund is a distinct, pAsset-denominated stream. Exact values are finalized closer to launch.

## Parameters

Current yield-related values and production-finality status live on [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/parameters). This page owns the source model, not the numeric table.

---

[pUSD: No Counterparty, No Ceiling](https://polarisfinance.io/blog/pusd-no-counterparty/)
How pUSD delivers counterparty-free yield that scales with protocol adoption — and the ecosystem taking shape around it.

Relevant app/search vocabulary: Earn, earn, yield, APR, Stability Pool, deposit, claim rewards.

---

## Polaris Glossary

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources

# Polaris Glossary

Every key Polaris term, alphabetized. Each entry is one or two sentences and links to its full treatment where one exists. For the narrative map of how the pieces fit, read [Core Concepts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/core-concepts).

## Live app terms

These labels match the current testnet app and route users to the glossary entry plus the matching guide page.

App term | Glossary entry | Guide

Advanced | [Advanced](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#a) | [Advanced app tools](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/advanced)
APR | [APR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#a) | [Earn](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/earn)
Borrow / CDP | [CDP](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#c), [Trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#t) | [Borrow](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/borrow)
Earn / SP | [Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#s), [SP](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#s) | [Earn](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/earn)
Floor Carry | [Floor Carry](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#f) | [Split](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/split)
fpETH / vpETH | [fpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#f), [vpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#v) | [Split](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/split)
Guide | [Guide](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#g) | [Polaris App](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app)
POLAR Staking | [POLAR Staking](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#p) | [POLAR token](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar)
PSM / Redemption | [PSM](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#p), [Redemption](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#r) | [Advanced](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/advanced)
Reserve Loan | [Reserve Loan](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#r) | [Advanced](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/advanced)
Split | [Split](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#s) | [Split](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/split)
Swap / WETH | [WETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#w), [pETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#p) | [Swap](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/swap)
Zap | [Zap](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#z) | [Zap](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/zap)

## A-Z jump

[A](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#a) · [B](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#b) · [C](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#c) · [F](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#f) · [G](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#g) · [I](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#i) · [L](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#l) · [M](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#m) · [O](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#o) · [P](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#p) · [R](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#r) · [S](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#s) · [T](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#t) · [V](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#v) · [W](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#w) · [Z](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#z)

## A

**Advanced**
The app area for manual or lower-level tools such as approvals, PSM/redemption actions, reserve loans, conversion, and POLAR Staking controls. Use it only after verifying the contract, function, token, amount, and quote; see [Advanced](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/advanced).

**APR (Annual Percentage Rate)**
An annualized rate estimate, commonly shown for borrow cost or yield. APR is not a promised return: realized cost or yield can change with rates, activity, liquidations, fees, execution, and market conditions.

## B

**Bonding curve**
The two-way curve that backs [pETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth). It enforces a mathematical relationship between pETH supply and price (minting raises it, burning lowers it), providing a contract-native buy/sell path with no liquidity providers and a [floor price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price) designed to rise under the curve invariants. See [The Bonding Curve](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth).

## C

**CDP (Collateralized Debt Position)**
The mechanism behind minting: post collateral, mint a pAsset directly from the protocol rather than borrowing another user's funds. Each position is an isolated [trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#t). See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

**Conversion auction**
The mechanism connecting pETH and POLAR: it burns [pETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth) to mint new [POLAR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar). The most protocol-synergetic action possible: every conversion raises the floor and releases ETH yield. See [Conversion Auctions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions).

**Converter rebate**
A stewardship-controlled percentage of ETH returned to the converter in a [conversion auction](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions). The dial between conversion volume and system yield.

## F

**Fee Router**
Receives pETH from conversions and bonding-curve swap-fee buybacks, buffers it over a reward period, and distributes pETH rewards to a stewardship-designated receiver — currently troves, pro-rata to debt. Staker yield is distributed separately, in pAsset terms, via the interest router. See [Stewardship](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship).

**Floor Carry**
The possible return from holding [fpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#f) while the pETH [floor price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#f) rises. It is not a fixed APR or a guarantee; it depends on actual floor growth, market pricing, execution costs, and the redemption path remaining available.

**Floor price**
The contract-defined minimum price of [pETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth), in ETH terms, under the bonding-curve invariants. It is designed to move upward during normal protocol operation and represents the modeled *last person out* price if everyone exited at once, subject to contract correctness, Ethereum liveness, gas, slippage, and MEV. See [The Floor Price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price).

**Floor ratio**
The floor price divided by the market price, as a percentage. It frames the curve-implied drawdown before gas, slippage, MEV, smart-contract risk, and chain-liveness risk. See [The Floor Price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price).

**Flows**
The protocol's internal value movements (fees, conversions, yield distribution), routed by the [Fee Router](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#f). Inward-facing flows sustain the core; outward-facing flows grow the ecosystem (see *Inward-facing / Outward-facing*).

**fpETH**
The floor-price leg created when [pETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/split) is split into fpETH and [vpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#v). fpETH can be redeemed for WETH at the pETH floor-price value under the splitter and bonding-curve assumptions, and it is used as reserve-loan collateral.

## G

**Guide**
The app area that routes users by goal, such as getting pETH, borrowing pAssets, earning yield, splitting pETH, or verifying safety. See [Polaris App](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app).

## I

**ICR (Individual Collateralization Ratio)**
The collateralization ratio of a single [trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#t): the value of its pETH collateral divided by its pAsset debt. If a trove's ICR falls below the minimum, it can be [liquidated](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/liquidations).

**Inward-facing / Outward-facing**
The design split deciding which token handles what. *Inward-facing* flows (interest rates, floor growth, Stability Pool incentivization, liquidations) are handled by pETH and the pAssets. *Outward-facing* growth (external liquidity, Flow onboarding and weights, ecosystem incentives) is handled by [POLAR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar).

## L

**Liquidation**
The process that closes an undercollateralized [trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#t): when its [ICR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#i) drops below the minimum, the [Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield) absorbs its debt and receives its collateral. See [Liquidations](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/liquidations).

## M

**Minting (mechanism)**
The inverse of [redemption](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#r): when a pAsset trades *above* peg (e.g. pUSD > \$1), arbitrageurs add pETH to troves to mint the pAsset and sell it, pushing the price down. This restores the ceiling and is one of the onchain signals used to set interest rates. See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

**Minting (verb)**
Creating a pAsset by opening a [trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#t) and drawing pAsset debt against pETH collateral. Polaris *mints* pAssets, not "issues" or "creates" them.

## O

**Oracle**
An onchain feed Polaris is expected to use to price ETH collateral. Peg awareness needs no oracle: redemption and minting volume serve as an internal price signal for each pAsset. The final provider and feed address are forthcoming; Public Testnet 1 uses Sepolia and production is intended for Ethereum mainnet. See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting), [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status), and [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts).

## P

**pAsset**
A Polaris synthetic debt asset minted from a CDP — pUSD, pGOLD, pCHF, and beyond. Each is overcollateralized by pETH and counterparty-free. pETH itself is the collateral token, not CDP-minted pAsset debt. See [The pAsset Catalog](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/passet-catalog).

**pBigMac**
A novelty pAsset demonstrating the "mint anything" thesis: if a price feed exists, Polaris can mint a pAsset that tracks it.

**pCHF**
The Polaris synthetic Swiss franc — a pAsset pegged to CHF.

**pETH**
ETH deposited into the two-way [bonding curve](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth). The *only* collateral Polaris accepts: yield-bearing, with a [floor price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price) designed to rise under the curve invariants. Volatile against ETH, with a contract-defined floor under stated assumptions. See [The Bonding Curve](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth).

**pGOLD**
The Polaris synthetic gold pAsset, designed to track gold without issuer custody or KYC at the base protocol layer. See [The pAsset Catalog](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/passet-catalog).

**Pathfinders**
Core believers, not just early followers. Access is earned through merit (explanatory threads, deep research, finding Easter eggs) and rewarded with early alphas, testnet access, and dev-stage updates.

**Polar Council**
A planned treasury-funded program for onboarding and ecosystem contributors, similar in spirit to veAERO's Flight School. Its final allocation, eligibility, operating framework, and reporting rules are pending publication.

**POLAR**
The protocol's stewardship token and outward-facing growth lever. After the initial distribution, new POLAR can be minted *only* by burning pETH in a [conversion auction](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions). See [The POLAR Token](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar).

**POLAR Staking**
The current app staking surface for staking POLAR and claiming routed rewards where the deployment exposes it. It is distinct from forthcoming [vePOLAR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#v) locking, which adds lock durations, vote-power decay, and stewardship rights.

**PolarEX**
Polaris' integrated DEX. Its swap fees are one of the system's yield sources.

**PSM (Peg Stability Module)**
The contract/module that handles pAsset minting and redemption mechanics for a pAsset instance. Use PSM or redemption actions only after checking the pAsset, fee, output amount, and contract address.

**pUSD**
The flagship pAsset, designed to track the US dollar and be backed by pETH collateral. Intended to be counterparty-free and fully onchain. See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

## R

**Recovery Mode**
A protocol-wide protective state (Emergency Mode in the contracts) triggered when a pAsset's system-wide reserve-to-debt ratio (the [TCR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#t)) falls below a fixed threshold. While active, operations that reduce a trove's collateral or increase its debt are restricted (they must clear a higher minimum ratio and may not lower an existing trove's ICR); liquidation eligibility is unchanged. See [Recovery Mode](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/recovery-mode).

**Redemption**
Exchanging a pAsset for its underlying collateral at face value (minus a fee) when the pAsset trades *below* peg. Redemptions restore the floor and are one of the onchain signals used to set interest rates. See [Redemptions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations).

**Reserve Loan**
A WETH loan against [fpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#f) floor-value collateral. It is separate from a pAsset trove: reserve loans use the pETH floor leg and reserve accounting, while troves mint pAssets against pETH collateral.

## S

**sepETH**
Sepolia ETH, the testnet-native gas asset used on Public Testnet 1. It has no mainnet ETH value; when wrapped for app flows it may appear as WETH.

**SP (Stability Pool)**
Common abbreviation for the [Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#s), the pAsset first-loss pool that absorbs liquidations and pays depositors borrower-interest share and liquidation gains.

**Split**
The app flow and contract operation that turns pETH into equal amounts of [fpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#f) and [vpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#v), or merges equal fpETH and vpETH amounts back into pETH. See [pETH Split](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/split).

**Spike-and-decay**
The pricing model for [conversion auctions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions): the conversion price continuously decays until a conversion *spikes* it upward, proportionally to the POLAR supply that conversion inflated.

**Stability Pool**
A reserve of pAssets that absorbs the debt of [liquidated](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/liquidations) troves and is the protocol's primary venue for earning yield. See [The Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield).

**StablecoinOS**
The CDP-factory layer: many distinct pAssets, each issued by its own CDP instance, all sharing one pETH collateral and bonding curve. See [Core Concepts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/core-concepts).

**Stewardship**
The bounded role stewards play: tuning *quantitative parameters within the bounds the contracts define*. The immutable core cannot be changed; stewards steward, they do not govern. See [Stewardship, not Governance](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship).

## T

**TCR (Total Collateralization Ratio)**
The system-wide health metric: the bonding-curve reserve backing total collateral, valued in the pAsset, divided by total pAsset debt (the contract's reserve-to-debt ratio). Not a plain market-value-of-collateral over debt figure. Dropping below the configured threshold triggers [Recovery Mode](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/recovery-mode).

**Trove**
An individual borrowing position in a Polaris CDP. Each trove is isolated, with its own collateral, debt, and [ICR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#i). Polaris uses *trove*, never "vault" or "position". See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

## V

**vePOLAR**
The forthcoming vote-escrow layer for POLAR — POLAR locked for [stewardship](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship) rights and a share of protocol yield. It is distinct from current POLAR Staking; the vePOLAR locking mechanism, contract address, lock durations, and vote-power rules are pending audited deployment. See [vePOLAR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship/vepolar).

**vpETH**
The volatility-premium leg created when [pETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/split) is split into [fpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources#f) and vpETH. vpETH represents exposure to pETH's market value above the floor and can lose significant value if the premium compresses.

## W

**WETH**
Wrapped ETH, the ERC-20 form of ETH used by app contracts and testnet flows. On Sepolia, WETH is a test token backed by testnet ETH, not mainnet ETH.

## Z

**Zap**
An app flow that bundles multiple steps, such as WETH -> pUSD or pGOLD -> Stability Pool deposit. A zap is an execution shortcut, not a risk reducer; check approvals, simulation, slippage, quote freshness, and first-loss risk. See [Zap](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/zap).

---

Missing a term? The narrative version of every concept lives on its dedicated page, starting from [Core Concepts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/core-concepts); the long-form essays are on the [Polaris blog](https://polarisfinance.io/blog/).

Relevant app/search vocabulary: app, official app, testnet, Sepolia, WETH, WETH faucet, Zap, Split, Swap, Borrow, Earn, Guide, Advanced, Analytics, APR, Reserve Loan.

---

## Polaris Audits & Security

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security

# Polaris Audits & Security

> **Pre-launch note:** Audits and verifications are ongoing; this page will be updated with major developments.

## The security model

Polaris's intended security model rests as much on what it *cannot* do as on what it can:

- **Immutability.** The core contracts are intended to be non-upgradeable after deployment. This removes upgrade-key and proxy-admin attack surface, raising the stakes on getting the code right: immutability cuts both ways — it cannot be tampered with, and it cannot be patched.
- **No admin keys.** The design has no freeze, blacklist, or pause functions for anyone, including the team. No privileged key is intended to move user funds.
- **Bounded stewardship.** Stewards adjust only quantitative parameters — many of them bounded by hardcoded ranges (for example interest-rate maxima, fee-split floors, and the mint/redemption decay factors) — and cannot reach the core logic. See [Stewardship, not Governance](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship).
- **Narrow oracle dependency.** Peg awareness uses internal redemption and minting volume rather than a pAsset price oracle. Collateral pricing still requires an ETH price feed; the provider, feed address, and network details are not published until launch.

For the full accounting of what can still go wrong, read the [Risk Disclosure](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure).

## Bug bounty & responsible disclosure

A bug-bounty program and responsible-disclosure process are intended to run from launch. Once live, this section will document:

- **Intake.** The single official channel for submitting a report, linked from this page. Until it is published, there is no official intake; do not send exploit details to private forms, DMs, or third-party bounty pages unless linked from these docs.
- **Scope.** Which contracts, networks, and surfaces are in scope, and what is explicitly out of scope.
- **Severity tiers and rewards.** How findings are classified and what each tier pays.
- **Disclosure process.** The expected steps and timeline from report to fix to public disclosure, plus safe-harbor terms for good-faith research.

> **Note:** Until the program and intake appear here, treat the protocol as having no public bounty. Do not trust a bounty URL, reward table, or disclosure form found anywhere else.

## Reporting a vulnerability

The formal disclosure channel will be published alongside the bug-bounty program. Until then, use only public Polaris channels to ask for the current intake path; do not send exploit details to private forms, direct messages, or third-party bounty pages unless linked from these docs.

---

For the full launch checklist, see [Polaris Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status) and [Safety and Verification](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification). For production contract addresses (also forthcoming), see [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts).

---

## Polaris Brand Assets

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/brand-assets

# Polaris Brand Assets

Everything you need to represent Polaris correctly: logos, palette, and type. The canonical surface is dark: Polaris was designed against a night sky, not a white page.

Image: Polaris full logo

*Full logo preview for placement checks; keep the SVG unmodified and provide enough contrast around it.*

## Logos

The logo files live at the site root and link directly:

Asset | Path | Use

Emblem | `/emblem.svg` | Standalone mark, for avatars, favicons, tight spaces
Full logo | `/full-logo.svg` | Mark plus wordmark, for headers and primary placements
Favicon (SVG) | `/favicon.svg` | Scalable browser favicon
Favicon (PNG / ICO) | `/favicon.png`, `/favicon.ico` | Raster favicons for legacy contexts
Apple touch icon | `/apple-touch-icon.png` | iOS home-screen icon

## Palette — Celestial Night Sky

### Navy backgrounds

The canonical surfaces. Layer from darkest to lighter for depth.

Swatch | Name | Hex | Role | Accessible use

Navy darkest | `#050a14` | Primary background | Use with Star or Star bright text.
Navy dark | `#0a1628` | Raised surfaces, panels | Use with Star text for body copy.
Navy | `#0d1f3c` | Cards, borders, accents | Use as a surface or border color, not low-contrast body text.

### Star and cream

The light values: text and starlight against the navy.

Swatch | Name | Hex | Role | Accessible use

Star | `#E8DCC4` | Primary text, default starlight | Primary body text on navy backgrounds.
Star bright | `#FAF6EE` | Brightest highlights, emphasis | Use sparingly for high-emphasis text and icons.
Cream | `#F5F0E6` | Secondary light surfaces | Pair with dark navy text; do not use as text on white.

### Token colors

Each core asset has a signature color.

Swatch | Token | Hex | Accessible use

pUSD | `#E8DCC4` | Works as text/icon color on navy; add text labels in charts.
pETH | `#7BA5C9` | Works for lines and icons on navy; avoid color-only legends.
POLAR | `#9B8FCF` | Works for accent graphics on navy; pair with visible labels.

Do not rely on color alone. Token charts, route maps, and warnings should pair these colors with labels, icons, or table text so the meaning survives in grayscale, print, and assistive technologies.

## Typography

Role | Typeface | Notes

Headings | **Cormorant Garamond** | Serif. Sets the cerebral, classical tone.
Body | **Inter** | Sans-serif. Clean and legible at every size.

## Usage

**Do**

- Treat **dark mode as the canonical surface**: design against the navy palette first.
- Use **Star (`#E8DCC4`)** for body text, not pure white.
- Keep **borders subtle**: low-contrast navy edges, never heavy outlines.
- Give the emblem clear space; let it breathe.

**Don't**

- Don't use **pure white (`#FFFFFF`)** for text — too harsh against the navy. Use Star or Star bright.
- Don't recolor the logos or the token colors.
- Don't stretch, rotate, or add effects to the marks.
- Don't place the logo on a busy or low-contrast background.

---

Need the terms behind the tokens? See the [Glossary](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources).

---

## Polaris Contracts & Addresses

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts

# Polaris Contracts & Addresses

This page owns contract addresses and verification rules. Public Testnet 1 addresses are Sepolia artifacts and may be redeployed. Production addresses are not published yet.

Do not use any claimed production address unless it appears here after Launch Status says production is live.

## Source and freshness

Field | Value

Last verified | June 8, 2026
Network | Sepolia
Chain ID | `11155111`
Address source | Public Testnet 1 internal Sepolia manifests listed on [Public Testnet 1 Artifacts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet)
Public artifact commit/source URL | Not published in these docs during Public Testnet 1
Explorer verification pattern | Open `https://sepolia.etherscan.io/address/` and match the address exactly

## Deployment shape

Polaris has one shared core plus one instance set per pAsset.

Layer | What deploys

Shared core | pETH/bonding curve, splitter, POLAR/ShareSystem, Fee Router, stewardship, staking, reserve loans, reserve token
Per-pAsset instance | pAsset token, CDP manager, CDP NFT, Stability Pool, internal PSM, interest controller, price feeds, read helper

## Polaris Finance FAQ

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/faq

# Polaris Finance FAQ

FAQ answers are intentionally short. Follow the linked canonical page when you need exact status, addresses, parameters, or risk details.

## General

### What is Polaris?

Polaris is a pETH-powered stablecoin operating system. ETH becomes pETH through a bonding curve, pETH collateralizes pAssets such as pUSD and pGOLD, and protocol activity routes yield through onchain mechanisms. Start with [Core Concepts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/core-concepts).

### What is live now?

The current phase is owned by [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status). As of June 2026, Polaris is in Public Testnet 1 on Sepolia (`11155111`), and production/mainnet use is not live.

### Which app should I use?

Use only the app listed on [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status). For Public Testnet 1, follow [Public Testnet Quickstart](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/quickstart).

## Using the app

### How do I get started on testnet?

Open the official testnet app, connect Sepolia, use test assets only, swap into pETH, then try Borrow, Earn, Split, Zap, or Advanced as needed. The step-by-step path is [Public Testnet Quickstart](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/quickstart).

### Why does the app show Borrow if docs say mint?

The app says Borrow because the user action creates debt. The mechanism is a CDP/trove: you deposit pETH collateral and mint a pAsset directly from the protocol. See [Borrow](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/borrow) and [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

### What should I do if a quote or transaction fails?

Use [Troubleshooting](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/troubleshooting). Most failures come from wrong network, stale quotes, insufficient allowance, insufficient gas, Recovery Mode restrictions, or changed state before inclusion.

## Contracts, parameters, and safety

### Where are contract addresses?

[Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts) is the address owner. Public Testnet 1 addresses are Sepolia artifacts; production addresses are not published until Launch Status says production is live.

### Where are current parameters?

[Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/parameters) owns numeric values. Task pages link there instead of repeating tables.

### Where are testnet manifests?

[Public Testnet 1 Artifacts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet) owns manifest paths and testnet artifact sources.

### What should I verify before signing?

Use [Safety & Verification](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification). At minimum, verify frontend URL, network, chain ID, address, token, spender, amount, and action.

## Risk

### Can I lose funds?

Yes. Polaris involves smart-contract risk, collateral price risk, liquidation risk, Stability Pool first-loss risk, MEV risk, oracle risk, and frontend/phishing risk. Read [Risk Disclosure](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure).

### What is ICR?

ICR is Individual Collateralization Ratio: the health ratio of a single trove. See the [Glossary](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources) and [Managing Your Trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/managing-your-trove).

### What is TCR or Emergency Mode?

TCR is the system-level reserve-to-debt health metric for a pAsset. If it drops below the configured threshold, Recovery Mode tightens risk-increasing borrower actions. See [Recovery Mode](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/recovery-mode) and [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/parameters).

## Security

### Is Polaris audited?

Audit status is owned by [Audits & Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security). Until final reports are linked there, do not rely on audit claims found elsewhere.

### Is there a bug bounty?

Bug-bounty and disclosure status are owned by [Audits & Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security). Do not send exploit details to private forms, DMs, or third-party pages unless the official security page links them.

Relevant app/search vocabulary: app, official app, testnet, Sepolia, WETH, WETH faucet, Zap, Split, Swap, Borrow, Earn, Guide, Advanced, Analytics, APR, Reserve Loan.

---

## Polaris Parameters

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/parameters

# Polaris Parameters

This page owns current numeric Polaris parameters. Task and concept pages should link here instead of repeating parameter tables; when a numeric value is necessary, this page is the source of truth.

Values below are Public Testnet 1 values last reviewed on June 8, 2026. They are replaceable Sepolia values, not production constants. Production parameters are not published unless this page and Launch Status say they are.

## Source and freshness

Field | Value

Network | Sepolia
Chain ID | `11155111`
Last verified | June 8, 2026
Artifact source | [Public Testnet 1 Artifacts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet)

Production finality | Pending; do not infer production constants from Sepolia values

## Shared core parameters

Parameter | Public Testnet 1 value | Source

Bonding curve alpha | `193218996973422650` | Core manifest
Bonding curve beta | `150000000000000000` (15%) | Core manifest

## pAsset instance parameters

Parameter | pUSD | pGOLD | Source

Minimum collateral ratio (MCR) | 115% | 115% | Manifest
Emergency Mode action MCR | 150% | 150% | Manifest
Emergency Mode trigger ratio | 110% | 110% | Manifest
Mint fee floor | 0.05% | 0.05% | Deploy script
Redemption fee floor | 0.05% | 0.05% | Deploy script
Mint alpha | `1e18` | `1e18` | Deploy script
Redemption alpha | `1e18` | `1e18` | Deploy script
Mint minute decay factor | `998076443575628800` | `998076443575628800` | Deploy script
Redemption minute decay factor | `998076443575628800` | `998076443575628800` | Deploy script

## How to use this page

- Use the app quote and live contract reads before signing any transaction.
- Do not copy Sepolia values into a production integration.
- If a task page and this page disagree, treat this page as the parameter owner and verify against the current manifest.
- Treat older beta, MCR, or Recovery Mode examples as stale if they disagree with the values above.

Relevant app/search vocabulary: app, official app, testnet, Sepolia, WETH, WETH faucet, Zap, Split, Swap, Borrow, Earn, Guide, Advanced, Analytics, APR, Reserve Loan.

---

## Polaris Risk Disclosure

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure

# Polaris Risk Disclosure

Polaris is trustless, immutable infrastructure. That design choice has real costs, and we disclose them before we claim the benefits. Every risk below is paired with the mechanism that mitigates it (*mitigates*, not eliminates). Read this page in full before you mint, deposit, or lock anything.

> **Note:** Nothing here is financial advice. Polaris is currently in the phase shown on [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status). Testnet assets have no monetary value, and production use is not live unless Launch Status says it is. Trustlessness means the protocol will not, and cannot, step in to make you whole.

## Smart-contract risk

Polaris lives entirely onchain. Its bonding curve, CDPs, Stability Pool, and conversion auctions are smart contracts, and any smart contract can contain bugs.

**Mitigation.** The intended core is immutable and admin-key-free, removing upgrade and governance-key attack surface but raising the stakes on getting the code right. Final audit reports are not published in these docs yet; until reports appear on [Audits & Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security), treat the protocol as unaudited. Immutability cuts both ways: it cannot be tampered with, and it cannot be patched. Residual risk is never zero.

## Collateral and price risk

The only collateral is [pETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth), which is **volatile against ETH**. pETH trades at a varying premium above its [floor price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price), and that premium can compress sharply in a drawdown.

**Mitigation.** The [floor ratio](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price) frames your curve-implied downside in ETH terms before execution and technical risk: a 90% floor ratio means 10% drawdown to the floor, a 67% floor ratio means 33% drawdown. Your risk is set at *entry*: the spread between your entry price and the floor. Under the intended curve invariant, the floor rises during normal operation; it is still dependent on functioning contracts and live Ethereum execution.

> **Warning:** A high floor ratio does not protect against ETH's own volatility. pETH's floor is denominated in ETH terms. If ETH itself falls against the dollar, your pETH falls with it.

## Oracle risk

Pricing pETH collateral for liquidation requires an ETH price feed. A stale, manipulated, or failed oracle could misprice collateral and trigger wrong liquidations, or fail to trigger correct ones.

**Mitigation.** Peg awareness needs no oracle: redemption and minting volume act as the protocol's internal price signal for each pAsset (see [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting)). The oracle dependency is meant to be confined to pricing the *collateral*, ETH. Current Sepolia testnet price-feed and medianiser addresses are listed on [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet); final production providers and feed addresses are forthcoming. Do not treat any production oracle address as official until it is listed in [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts).

## Peg and depeg risk

A pAsset can trade away from its peg, below it in a panic or above it in a demand spike. Depegs can be temporary or, under severe stress, sustained.

**Mitigation.** Two opposing arbitrage mechanisms defend the peg structurally:

- **[Redemptions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations)** make it profitable to buy the pAsset below peg and redeem it for collateral, pushing the price up toward \$1.
- **Minting** makes it profitable to mint and sell the pAsset above peg, pushing the price back down.

Because both pay arbitrageurs the moment the peg slips, they activate without anyone's permission. pUSD is a counterparty-free stablecoin in the same trust-minimized family as LUSD and BOLD, so the peg is not propped up by a redeemable offchain reserve; it is held by onchain incentives.

## Liquidation risk (borrowers)

If your [trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting)'s [Individual Collateralization Ratio (ICR)](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources) falls below the minimum, the trove is [liquidated](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/liquidations): you lose your collateral to cover the debt.

**Mitigation.** Liquidation logic is transparent and auditable onchain, with no discretionary calls. You control your ICR: post more collateral, or repay debt, to stay clear of the threshold. pETH's rising floor and yield-bearing nature dampen, but do not remove, the volatility that causes liquidations. Monitor your trove actively, especially in volatile markets.

## Stability Pool first-loss risk (depositors)

Depositing pAssets into the [Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield) is the primary way to earn yield, but the Pool is the system's **first-loss buffer**. When a trove is liquidated, your deposited pAssets are burned to cancel its debt, and you receive the liquidated pETH collateral in exchange.

**Mitigation.** Liquidations are designed to occur while the trove is still overcollateralized, so depositors typically receive collateral worth *more* than the pAssets they gave up, and that surplus is the source of the Pool's yield. The risk is the opposite case: a liquidation processed below the value of the burned deposit, or a sharp collapse in the received collateral's value. You take on liquidation-processing risk in exchange for yield; size your deposit accordingly.

## Recovery Mode

When the system-wide reserve backing relative to total debt (the [reserve-to-debt ratio, shown as the TCR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources)) drops below a threshold, the protocol enters [Recovery Mode](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/recovery-mode), a defensive state with tighter requirements.

**Mitigation.** Recovery Mode is a feature, not a failure: it raises the bar for the whole system to rebuild collateralization quickly. Borrowers bear the cost: in Recovery Mode you cannot withdraw collateral or take on more debt unless you clear a higher minimum ratio and do not lower your ICR (pure collateral top-ups and repayments stay allowed). Liquidation eligibility itself does not change; it stays at the normal minimum in every mode. Keep your ICR comfortably above the minimum so a stress event does not box in your ability to act.

## Stewardship and economic risk

Stewards control quantitative parameters: the interest splits, conversion-price parameters, and the [converter rebate](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources). Parameter changes can affect your yield and your position's economics.

**Mitigation.** Stewardship is *constrained*: many economic parameters move only within hardcoded ranges (for example the converter rebate and the interest-split floors), and the core logic cannot be touched at all (see [Stewardship, not Governance](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship)). Some conversion-price parameters are not yet bounded in the current prototype; instead, conversion issuance is self-throttled by its [spike-and-decay pricing](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions), so large or rapid minting limits itself. What remains is ordinary parameter risk, not the unbounded-governance risk that has denatured other protocols. Final per-parameter bounds are pending audited deployment.

## MEV risk

Redemptions, liquidations, and [conversion auctions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions) are profitable, permissionless actions, so they attract MEV searchers and arbitrage bots. Ordinary users competing by hand will usually lose the race to automation.

**Mitigation.** This is by design: MEV competition keeps the peg tight and the floor rising, because searchers do the protocol's stabilizing work for profit. Do not expect to win arbitrage races manually; interact with the protocol for the position you want (a trove, a Stability Pool deposit, a pETH hold), not to front-run the bots.

## Dependency risk (Ethereum)

Polaris is currently in Public Testnet 1 on Sepolia, and production is intended for Ethereum mainnet (chain ID `1`) once launched. Production Polaris inherits Ethereum's risks: consensus failures, severe congestion, or a contentious fork.

**Mitigation.** This dependency is deliberate and unhedged: the only counterparties Polaris is meant to ask you to trust are immutable code and Ethereum itself. We do not spread across chains to dilute that exposure, because doing so would reintroduce the bridge and external-dependency risks Polaris exists to avoid.

## Frontend and phishing risk

Fake frontends, fake contract addresses, and fake support channels can appear before users know what the official ones look like. A malicious frontend can ask you to approve tokens, sign dangerous messages, or connect to the wrong network.

**Mitigation.** For Public Testnet 1, use only [https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/) on Sepolia (`11155111`) and only with no-value test assets. Use no production app URL, production network, production contract address, audit claim, or bug-bounty link unless it is listed in these docs. Never enter a seed phrase, private key, or recovery words into any Polaris-related site. The current status checklist lives on [Polaris Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status), [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet), and [Safety and Verification](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification).

---

For the reasoning behind these trade-offs, read [Why Polaris?](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/why-polaris). For the precise definition of any term above, see the [Glossary](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources).

---

## Polaris Safety & Verification

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification

# Polaris Safety & Verification

Use this checklist before connecting a wallet, approving a token, or signing a transaction.

## Verification checklist

Check | Required source

Current phase and official app | [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status)
Public Testnet 1 quickstart | [Public Testnet Quickstart](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/quickstart)
Network and chain ID | [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status)
Contract addresses | [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts)
Numeric parameters | [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/parameters)
Testnet manifest paths | [Public Testnet 1 Artifacts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet)
Risk model | [Risk Disclosure](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure)
Audit, bounty, and disclosure status | [Audits & Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security)

## Things Polaris will not ask for

- Your seed phrase, private key, or recovery words.
- A signature from an unofficial frontend.
- Mainnet funds for Public Testnet 1.
- A production address before [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status) says production is live.
- A bug-bounty or disclosure form not linked from [Audits & Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security).

## Wallet prompt checks

Before signing, verify:

1. The app URL is the official URL for the current phase.
2. The wallet network is the intended network.
3. The token, amount, spender, and receiving contract match the action.
4. The address appears on [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts).
5. The transaction matches the action tab you are using.

If any item fails, reject the signature and use [Troubleshooting](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/troubleshooting) before trying again.

Relevant app/search vocabulary: official app, app URL, phishing, verify contracts, testnet, mainnet.

---

## Polaris Public Testnet 1 Artifacts

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet

# Polaris Public Testnet 1 Artifacts

This page owns Public Testnet 1 artifact sources: network, manifest paths, and testnet caveats. Addresses live on [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts). Numeric values live on [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/parameters).

## Environment

Fact | Value

Public phase | Public Testnet 1
Last verified | June 8, 2026
Network | Sepolia
Chain ID | `11155111`
Official app | [https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/)
Testnet value | No monetary value, no production redemption, no mainnet claim
Testnet gas | Sepolia ETH
Reserve token | Mock WETH/reserve token from the app faucet when enabled
Active pAssets | pUSD and pGOLD
Illustrative or future pAssets | pCHF, pBigMac, and other catalog examples

## Manifest locations

Protocol artifact source and commit are not public in these docs during Public Testnet 1. The relative paths below identify the internal Sepolia manifests used for the published address and parameter tables; external readers should treat [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts) and [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/parameters) as the public source until a repository URL and commit are published.

Manifest | What it covers | Internal protocol path

Core manifest | Reserve token, pETH/bonding curve, POLAR/share system, staking, fee router, reserve loans, stewardship, splitter, vpToken | `deployments/sepolia/deployment-manifest.core.ts`
pUSD manifest | pUSD token, CDP manager, Stability Pool, PSM, price feed, medianiser, interest controller, read helper | `deployments/sepolia/deployment-manifest.pusd.ts`
pGOLD manifest | pGOLD token, CDP manager, Stability Pool, PSM, price feed, ETH/USD and XAU/USD medianisers, interest controller, read helper | `deployments/sepolia/deployment-manifest.pgold.ts`
Deploy script defaults | Fee floors, fee alpha values, decay factors, initial rates, and deployment constants not all written into the manifests | `script/DeployPolaris.s.sol`

Treat these as testnet artifacts. They are useful for testing, indexer scaffolding, and UI verification, but they are not public provenance or production-canonical.

## Related canonical pages

Need | Page

Current live/not-live status | [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status)
Fastest testnet user flow | [Public Testnet Quickstart](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/quickstart)
Sepolia addresses | [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts)
Sepolia parameters | [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/parameters)
Safety checks before signing | [Safety & Verification](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification)

Relevant app/search vocabulary: app, official app, testnet, Sepolia, WETH, WETH faucet, Zap, Split, Swap, Borrow, Earn, Guide, Advanced, Analytics, APR, Reserve Loan.

---

## Changelog

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/changelog

# Changelog

This is a changelog for **this documentation**, not the Polaris protocol. It records what changed in these pages — structure, content, and accessibility — so returning readers can see what is new.
Entries follow [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com), use ISO dates (YYYY-MM-DD), and are listed newest first.

## [v0.1] - 2026-06-08

Initial Polaris documentation release for Public Testnet 1. This version establishes the app-task guides, protocol concept pages, and canonical reference pages needed to understand and safely test the system.

### Added

- Initial documentation structure for Introduction, Launch Status, Public Testnet Quickstart, Using the App, protocol mechanics, Resources, and Changelog.
- Public Testnet Quickstart, Troubleshooting, and Parameters pages.
- Expanded app-task coverage for Dashboard, Swap, Borrow, Manage Trove, Earn, Split, Zap, and Advanced.
- Point-of-decision risk notes on the action pages (open a trove, redemptions, Stability Pool deposits, conversions), each linking to the full Risk Disclosure.
- A POLAR tokenomics page covering distribution, allocations, and vesting.
- The Polaris system overview diagram on Core Concepts and the lineage diagram on Why Polaris, each with descriptive alt text, a caption, and a numbered text description so the meaning survives without the image.
- An `llms.txt` machine-readable export so agents can consume the docs programmatically.
- This documentation changelog.

### Changed

- Reorganized navigation around launch status, quickstart, app tasks, protocol concepts, and canonical references.
- Moved address ownership to Contracts & Addresses, parameter ownership to Parameters, and testnet artifact ownership to Public Testnet 1 Artifacts.
- Kept `/resources` as the glossary route; no dedicated category landing page was added.
- Enriched the six role paths from thin link lists into role-specific reading sequences, each with a "Who this is for" line, prerequisites, and a one-line note on why each step matters.
- Added audience signposting that routes users to the right starting point.
- Accessibility pass across the affected pages, including image alternatives and text descriptions for every embedded diagram.
- Trimmed over-long meta descriptions so search snippets don't truncate.
- Consolidated repeated cross-page explanations (conversion and floor mechanics, the stewardship "what it cannot do" list, Fee Router routing, the Recovery Mode interaction) so each concept is defined once and referenced elsewhere.
